title: Fermi Arc
summary: A Fermi arc is a surface-state locus in boundary momentum space whose endpoints lie at the projections of bulk Weyl nodes of opposite total charge.
assumptions: Bulk Weyl nodes project to distinct boundary momenta.
regime: Boundary spectrum of a Weyl semimetal.
scope: Lecture One surface-state discussion connected to bulk Weyl nodes.

source_anchors.notes: Lecture One introduces Fermi arcs as a boundary signature of bulk Weyl topology.

representation: Boundary-state locus in the surface Brillouin zone.
failure_modes: If projected nodes coincide, the arc picture can reorganize.
retrieval_hints: Use when a query asks how Weyl bulk topology appears at a boundary.
